description: Patient age at the time of the study visit.
Biological sex as recorded in the source studies.
Chest pain type: typical angina, atypical angina, non-anginal pain, or asymptomatic. Asymptomatic presentation is counter-intuitively one of the strongest predictors of disease in this dataset.
Resting systolic blood pressure on admission to hospital.

Serum cholesterol. A recorded value of 0 is not a real cholesterol reading (you cannot have zero serum cholesterol and be alive) -- it is a sentinel value some sites used for 'not measured'. Treated as missing, not as zero, everywhere in this project.
Resting electrocardiogram result: normal, ST-T wave abnormality, or probable/definite left ventricular hypertrophy.
Maximum heart rate achieved during the exercise stress test.
Exercise-induced angina (chest pain during the stress test).
ST depression induced by exercise relative to rest, an ECG marker of reduced blood flow to the heart muscle.
Slope of the peak exercise ST segment: upsloping (least concerning), flat, or downsloping (most concerning). Missing in about a third of rows -- see the data audit for why.
Number of major coronary vessels colored by fluoroscopy, an invasive test. Missing in the large majority of non-Cleveland rows (see audit) because most sites didn't perform this test.
Thallium stress test result: normal, fixed defect, or reversible defect. Also an invasive/specialized test, missing in about half the dataset for the same site-driven reason as `ca`.
Presence of heart disease, binarized here from the source `num` column (an angiographic severity scale 0-4) into absent (num=0) vs. present (num>=1).
